summary: Open-source CLI ships only an obfuscated bundle
details: The package advertises itself as an open-source GitHub Projects kanban CLI but ships a single ~1MB runtime file (dist/agent-flow.js) that is fully transformed with javascript-obfuscator (hex-mangled identifiers, rotating string array, decoder wrapper). Deobfuscation reveals standard utility libraries (chalk, debug, ms, depd, http-errors, statuses, iconv-lite) and the only outbound network destination is api.github.com, consistent with the declared purpose. No credential reads, no install-time lifecycle scripts, no remote code fetch, and no exfiltration endpoint were observed in the traced structure. The concern is transparency: obfuscating an open-source CLI defeats reviewability for installers and makes future malicious changes harder to detect, but no installer-harm behavior is currently present.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
